subsystem.function: Hand plate, wrist band, quick release, soft-contact zones, finger clearance

instruction: Design only this subsystem: P0 plan, part_tree children, 12-24 coordinate-bearing geometry_ops. Every evidence_feature must appear. Do not modify other subsystems or the global envelope.

criteria.good: Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access.
criteria.bad: Finger linkages are glued, buried, or represented only as a glove shell.
criteria.loop_feedback: Model per-finger cartridges with hinge pin holes, thimble sockets, and visible single-pin removal path.

criteria.good: Flexion-resistance actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row.
criteria.bad: Actuators are bonded or hidden without connector/service geometry.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add plug-in actuator pockets, connector openings, knuckle-row mounts, and service labels.

criteria.good: Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance.
criteria.bad: Rigid shell ignores don/doff, comfort, and finger clearance.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add wrist quick release, soft-contact interface zones, finger clearance, and cable raceway inspection.

criteria.good: Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles.
criteria.bad: Wires run taut across knuckles or terminate at rigid mounts with no relief, inviting fatigue failure.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add service loops and strain relief at each knuckle and wrist crossing, and state a flex-rated bend radius.

citable_sources.title: NASA Systems Engineering Handbook SP-2016-6105 Rev2
1800 Mechanical Movements, Devices and Appliances (Hiscox, 1899, public domain)
